vulnerability.title: Set Lockout Time for Failed Password Attempts
vulnerability.description: This rule configures the system to lock out accounts during a specified time period after a number of incorrect login attempts using pam_faillock.so. Ensure that the file /etc/security/faillock.conf contains the following entry: unlock_time=<interval-in-seconds> where interval-in-seconds is or greater. pam_faillock.so module requires multiple entries in pam files. These entries must be carefully defined to work as expected. In order to avoid any errors when manually editing these files, it is recommended to use the appropriate tools, such as authselect or authconfig, depending on the OS version. If unlock_time is set to 0, manual intervention by an administrator is required to unlock a user. This should be done using the faillock tool.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q pam; }; then var_accounts_passwords_pam_faillock_unlock_time='' if [ -f /usr/bin/authselect ]; then if ! authselect check; then echo " authselect integrity check failed. Remediation aborted! This remediation could not be applied because an authselect profile was not selected or the selected profile is not intact. It is not recommended to manually edit the PAM files when authselect tool is available. In cases where the default authselect profile does not cover a specific demand, a custom authselect profile is recommended." exit 1 fi authselect enable-feature with-faillock authselect apply-changes -b else AUTH_FILES=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth") for pam_file in "${A

triage_decision.reason: This finding configures account lockout time after failed password attempts using pam_faillock.so, which is a standard STIG hardening measure. The remediation uses authselect or authconfig tools to safely modify PAM files, reducing risk of lockout or system breakage. The unlock_time setting prevents indefinite account lockouts by allowing automatic unlocking after a specified interval.

review_feedback: The plan only creates /etc/security/faillock.conf with unlock_time=900 but doesn't handle the complete remediation process. The official remediation requires authselect integrity checks, platform detection, and proper PAM file modifications. Creating just the faillock.conf file without the full authselect-based remediation could lead to inconsistent PAM configuration and potential authentication failures.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed plan would resolve the vulnerability by creating /etc/security/faillock.conf with unlock_time=900. However, the plan is incomplete compared to the official remediation script which handles platform detection, authselect integrity checks, and proper PAM file modifications. The plan should also verify the file doesn't already exist and handle potential edge cases.
concerns: Plan only creates faillock.conf file but doesn't handle authselect integrity checks or platform-specific logic
No verification that authselect is available or that the system is in a supported state
Missing error handling and rollback considerations
suggested_improvements: Implement platform detection and authselect integrity checks before making changes
Add verification steps to ensure the configuration was applied correctly
Include error handling and rollback procedures
Consider using the full remediation script approach rather than manual file creation
